meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Tutorial Hell (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop building, start selling. Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell using Antigravity and Claude Code. A guide for AI Builders.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you force your code to confront market reality. By integrating automated SaaS pricing models directly into your MVP, you shift from passive learner to active founder. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing architecture and Claude Code to iterate on revenue-generating features, ensuring your product is built for profit.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks in the trenches, stitching together API calls and tweaking UI components, yet your project remains a ghost in a GitHub repository. This is the siren song of 'tutorial hell'—a cycle of endless learning without a single dollar of validation. The architecture of your code is sound, but your business logic is non-existent.

True founders don't wait for 'perfection' to monetize; they bake revenue into the foundation. By treating pricing as a technical requirement rather than an afterthought, you force yourself to build features that customers actually value. It is time to stop polishing pixels and start shipping a product that demands a credit card.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Identify the core metric of your SaaS (e.g., tokens, API calls, or seats) and map it to three distinct pricing tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing schema based on usage-based billing metrics. Include fields for tier name, monthly cost, and usage limits for a developer-focused AI tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Create a pricing.json config file defining Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers based on token usage limits.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration Architecture
steps.description: Build a secure backend route to handle session creation so you can begin accepting payments immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Express.js route for Stripe Checkout session creation that accepts a priceId and user email.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'npm install stripe && create stripe-service.ts to handle session checkout flows'

steps.title: Enforce Feature Gating
steps.description: Prevent non-paying users from accessing premium features by implementing middleware that checks subscription status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks if a user has an active Stripe subscription before allowing access to the /api/generate endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /ask 'Implement a subscription-gate middleware for my existing API routes'

steps.title: Automate Usage Tracking
steps.description: Ensure every AI request is logged and billed correctly to avoid revenue leakage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema and a log function to record token consumption per user ID for billing reconciliation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /run 'Create a usage-logger utility that updates the database every time an AI request is completed'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment and run a smoke test on the checkout flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a test script to simulate a Stripe checkout session and verify that the database updates user access status upon success.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /test 'Run e2e tests for the complete user checkout and access-gating flow'

pro_tips: Always price your lowest tier high enough to filter out 'tire-kickers' who will only drain your support resources.
Use usage-based billing to align your revenue growth directly with your users' success.
Never build an admin dashboard before you have at least three paying customers; use raw database queries instead.
Implement a 'grandfathering' clause for your first 10 users to create urgent social proof and early evangelists.
If users don't complain about your pricing, you have priced your product too low.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, shift your focus entirely to cold outreach and content marketing. Do not add a single new feature until your first paying customer asks for it.
content.conversion_block: Stop hiding behind your code and start building a business that scales. The market is waiting for your solution, but they won't pay for potential—they pay for utility. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
